sections.paragraph:
- Original: μέλος

- Transliteration: melos

- Phonetic: mel'-os

- Definition:

1. a member, limb: a member of the human body

a. of bodies given up to criminal intercourse, because they are as it were members belonging to the harlot's body

- Origin: of uncertain affinity

- TDNT entry: 13:15,6

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Neuter

- Strong's: Of uncertain affinity; a limb or part of the body: - member.
